title: Supertonic vs Google TTS quality benchmark for ja, vi, and en
blocker_detail: Supertonic ONNX model weights are not available in this environment. No synthesis was run. No audio pairs were generated. No ASR roundtrip metrics were computed. No MOS/ABX score sheets were collected. All quality metrics are null — not fabricated.
required_inputs: Supertonic ONNX model weights (several hundred MB, not in repo)
ort runtime with supertonic graph compatibility
50+ utterances per language corpus (ja, vi, en)
Google STT or equivalent ASR for roundtrip CER/WER

deferred_test_cases.description: Each utterance synthesized by Google and Supertonic

deferred_test_cases.reason: No audio pairs generated, no bilingual reviewer scheduled

deferred_test_cases.description: ASR roundtrip CER/WER for both engines
deferred_test_cases.reason: No synthesis output, no ASR pipeline configured

deferred_test_cases.description: Adversarial set: numerals, names, code-switching, URLs, emoji, expression tags

prerequisites: SUPERTONIC-01 closes with confidence 1.0 (currently 0.85, empirical blockers B-1..B-10 unresolved)
Supertonic build reproducible with model weights accessible

notes: Supertonic is an opt-in TTS candidate (not default). This issue is not a blocker for v1 or local MT default. Quality benchmark is required before any SUPERTONIC-11 default flip.
